title: EU Formally Approves €90 Billion Ukraine Loan and 20th Russia Sanctions Package
detail: The European Union formally approved its €90 billion ($106B) loan to Ukraine and its 20th package of economic sanctions against Russia on April 23, completing a breakthrough blocked for months by Hungary and Slovakia. Hungary's new Prime Minister Péter Magyar, who defeated Viktor Orbán in the April 12 election, lifted the veto as he had no intention of blocking EU funds not involving Hungarian money. The loan covers Ukraine's military and budgetary needs for 2026-2027, with half disbursed in 2026 and the remainder in 2027. The 20th sanctions package targets Russia's energy revenues — including groundwork for a future ban on maritime services for Russian crude oil — expands restrictions on the shadow tanker fleet, and adds 120 new individual listings. The diplomatic breakthrough was also enabled by Ukraine completing repairs to the Druzhba pipeline, restoring oil flow to Slovakia on April 23.

media.caption: EU formally approves €90 billion loan for Ukraine and 20th package of Russia sanctions

title: Prince Harry Makes Unannounced Visit to Kyiv in Show of Solidarity
detail: Prince Harry, Duke of Sussex, made an unannounced visit to Kyiv on April 23, stating he came 'to remind people at home and around the world what Ukraine is facing and to support the people and partners who are doing extraordinary work every day.' The visit attracted significant international media coverage and served as a high-profile solidarity gesture. Harry met with Ukrainian officials and humanitarian workers. The visit came as Ukraine's frontlines remained intense, with 159 combat engagements recorded on April 23 and Russia deploying 9,096 kamikaze drones and 73 airstrikes in 24 hours. Russia also struck Zhytomyr Oblast by drone that morning, killing one woman.

media.caption: Prince Harry visits Kyiv; 159 combat engagements recorded on April 23
